page: Amazon Web Services m4.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Xen hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor with an x86_64 architecture, delivering 64 virtual CPUs across 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system operates at a clock speed of 2.3 GHz and includes a 45 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or GPU hardware is bundled with this instance type.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance tradeoff between single-core and multi-core operations. Single-core performance is relatively weak, with stress-ng, Geekbench, and single-threaded compression tests scoring in the bottom 10%. Conversely, multi-core performance is average, placing in the middle 50% for stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput also fall within the average tier. Large-scale LLM text generation and prompt processing tasks show weaker performance, placing in the bottom 25%.

The instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. Given the balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio and average multi-core capabilities, the m4.16xlarge is suited for general-purpose workloads that can leverage parallel processing, such as web serving, database operations, and software compilation. However, it is less optimal for single-threaded tasks or intensive AI inference workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services m4.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor with 64 vCPUs, 32 physical cores, and 256.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the Xen hypervisor, this x86_64 platform lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. Benchmark results demonstrate a significant performance divergence: single-core metrics, including single-threaded compression and text processing, rank in the bottom 10%, whereas multi-core benchmarks, memory bandwidth, and Redis operations consistently place in the middle 50%. This hardware profile offers a balanced resource density suitable for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, and database operations, though it presents a clear tradeoff for single-threaded applications and modern large language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m4.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and a 25 Gbps network baseline. It offers average multi-core performance but weak single-core capabilities.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m4.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and average multi-core performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose platform offering high-core density and average multi-threaded performance for enterprise workloads without local storage.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores
256.0 GB of system memory with 4.0 GB per core allocation
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Xen hypervisor
Average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance
Suited for multi-threaded databases, web serving, and software compilation
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
